evals.prompt: A new player is joining the table next session. Their name is Jordan. Walk me through getting their voice profile set up so the transcription system can identify them.
evals.expected_output: Should reference shattered-audio enroll command, suggest recording 30+ seconds, mention config.yaml channel_priors if they'll sit near a specific mic, and suggest a test session to verify.

assertions.text: Mentions the shattered-audio enroll command with correct syntax
Recommends recording at least 30 seconds of speech for profile quality
Mentions config.yaml channel_priors for mic-to-speaker mapping
Suggests a verification step (test session or short recording)
Mentions the profile storage location (~/.config/shattered-audio/profiles/)

evals.prompt: I ran a live session last night and a lot of the speaker labels came back as UNKNOWN. How do I fix the existing transcript and improve future accuracy?
evals.expected_output: Should cover: manual correction of speaker labels in chunk markdown, shattered-audio retrain command, adjusting actor_threshold, re-enrolling with longer samples, checking speaker_confidence scores in frontmatter.

assertions.text: Explains how to manually correct speaker labels in chunk markdown files
Mentions the shattered-audio retrain command for updating profiles from corrections
Mentions adjusting actor_threshold as a tuning lever
Notes that short utterances (<2s) are a known cause of UNKNOWN labels
Suggests re-enrolling with longer audio samples to improve profile quality
Mentions checking speaker_confidence in chunk frontmatter for diagnostics

evals.prompt: The cold pass keeps failing with a 403 error from HuggingFace. The hot pass works fine. What's going on?
evals.expected_output: Should identify this as the pyannote gated model issue, explain the HF_TOKEN requirement + model license acceptance at the HuggingFace page, mention that cold pass falls back gracefully if diarization is unavailable.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies this as a pyannote gated model access issue
Mentions the HF_TOKEN environment variable requirement
Directs user to accept the model license at the HuggingFace model page
Explains why hot pass works (uses whisper, no pyannote) while cold pass fails (uses pyannote diarization)
Mentions that cold pass can still work without diarization (graceful fallback)

evals.prompt: I'm the DM and I do voices for several NPCs — Thunk has a deep growly voice and Grigori speaks with a higher-pitched accent. How do I set up the transcription to tell these characters apart from my normal voice?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the actor/persona system: first enroll as actor with --dm flag, then enroll each character voice as a persona under the actor using --actor flag. Mention exemplar embeddings and prosody features.

assertions.text: Mentions enrolling the DM as an actor first with the --dm flag
Shows the --actor flag for enrolling character voices (personas) under the actor
Suggests enrolling each character voice separately with distinctive samples
Mentions that re-enrolling adds more exemplar embeddings for accuracy
Explains that distinctive pitch/energy differences help persona separation via prosody
Notes graceful degradation: if persona can't be distinguished, falls back to actor name

evals.prompt: The transcription correctly identifies me (Nick) but it never picks up when I'm doing Thunk's voice — it just labels everything as Nick. How do I improve persona detection?
evals.expected_output: Should cover persona debugging: check persona_threshold, persona_margin, prosody_weight settings, re-enroll Thunk with more exaggerated samples, use shattered-audio profiles to check prosody stats, consider that very similar voices may not separate.

assertions.text: Suggests checking and lowering persona_threshold for more aggressive detection
Recommends re-enrolling the persona with more distinctive voice samples
Mentions shattered-audio profiles command to view prosody stats
Suggests increasing prosody_weight if the character differs mainly in pitch/rate
Explains that d-vectors encode vocal tract identity — persona separation relies on prosodic differences

evals.prompt: I just finished session-ingest Pass 1 for session 12 and resolved UNKNOWN_3 to Perrin. The cold-pass chunks and WAVs are still in Inbox/. How do I use these corrections to improve voice profiles?
evals.expected_output: Should reference the --speaker-map flag on shattered-audio retrain, explain that it reads the speaker-map.md resolution table and remaps UNKNOWN labels during parsing, mention Pass 1b from session-ingest, and note that no manual editing of chunk files is needed.

assertions.text: Mentions the --speaker-map flag on shattered-audio retrain
Shows the correct command syntax with --speaker-map pointing to speaker-map.md
States that no manual find-replace or editing of chunk markdown files is needed
Mentions that only high and medium confidence resolutions are applied
References Pass 1b or the session-ingest integration workflow

evals.prompt: I want every session's speaker corrections to automatically improve my voice profiles. What's the workflow?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the end-to-end loop: session-ingest Pass 1 resolves speakers, Pass 1b feeds corrections into retrain via --speaker-map, profiles improve, next session has fewer UNKNOWNs. Mention keeping WAVs in Inbox/ until Pass 1b runs.

assertions.text: Describes the feedback loop: Pass 1 corrections → Pass 1b retrain → better future sessions
Mentions Pass 1b runs automatically after Pass 1 when cold-pass chunks exist
Notes the requirement to keep WAV files in Inbox/ until retrain completes
Recommends session-ingest path over manual correction for accuracy
Mentions --speaker-map flag as the bridge between the two systems
